headline: Google appeals US court ruling on search monopoly
summary: Alphabet's Google on Friday appealed a Washington federal judge's ruling ‌that it holds illegal monopolies in online search ‌and related advertising.  • Google argued that U.S. ​Judge Amit Mehta made legal errors in his 2024 ruling, which found the company illegally blocked competitors by paying billions of dollars annually to firms including Apple ‌to be the ⁠default search engine on new devices.  • The arrangements did not prevent the device ⁠makers and browser developers from promoting rival search services like Microsoft's Bing, Google argued.

headline: Maybe investors can wait to buy SpaceX after IPO: Lee Munson
summary: Elon Musk's private space operator, SpaceX (SPAX.PVT), released its S-1 filing this week as it plans to go public via IPO in June. The Wall Street Journal has reported that AI developer OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) is preparing to file for its own mega-IPO. As investors anticipate this mega-IPO, should they be equally cautious as they are bullish on these companies ? Portfolio Wealth Advisors president and CIO Lee Munson, Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre, and Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré weigh in on the wave of upcoming IPOs.

headline: What's Happening With IBM Stock?
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) surged 12.4% to close at $252.97 on May 21, 2026, after the Commerce Department announced $2.0 billion in quantum computing grants to nine companies. The headline? IBM secures $1 billion in government funding for a new quantum chip foundry called Anderon, with IBM matching the investment dollar-for-dollar. The stock's reaction was swift and decisive.

headline: Yotta CEO predicts $100 billion boom in India data centers
summary: Despite global skepticism around India’s AI ambitions and the absence of competitive foundational models, Sunil Gupta, Co-founder & CEO of Yotta Data Services says India’s AI ecosystem is nearing an inflection point, expecting the country to see a huge data center investment wave to the tune of $100 billion in the coming years. He highlights a new wave of startups building credible AI products, as well as the speed at which AI is being adopted by enterprises and government institutions.

raw.headline: Yotta CEO predicts $100 billion boom in India data centers
raw.summary: Despite global skepticism around India’s AI ambitions and the absence of competitive foundational models, Sunil Gupta, Co-founder & CEO of Yotta Data Services says India’s AI ecosystem is nearing an inflection point, expecting the country to see a huge data center investment wave to the tune of $100 billion in the coming years. He highlights a new wave of startups building credible AI products, as well as the speed at which AI is being adopted by enterprises and government institutions.
